title: Red Sox place Trevor Story on injured list with sports hernia, contemplating surgery
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox shortstop Trevor Story placed on 10-day injured list with sports hernia, decision on surgery uncertain.
Infielder Nick Sogard recalled from Triple-A Worcester to replace Story on the Red Sox roster.
Andruw Monasterio will play shortstop against the Braves; Marcelo Mayer remains at second base.

title: One driver dead in NH collision between pickup truck and school bus
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: David Peloski, 74, of Swanzey, NH, died after his pickup truck collided with a school bus in Marlborough.
The crash occurred on Route 12; 13 students on the bus were unharmed and later reunited with families.
School bus driver James French, 77, and several students were taken to hospitals with no serious injuries reported.

title: Carl Yastrzemski’s grandson, Braves’ Mike Yastrzemski, walks off Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Mike Yastrzemski hit a game-winning single in 10th inning as Braves defeated Red Sox 3-2.
Red Sox pitcher Connelly Early allowed two home runs over five innings, striking out six.
With the loss, Red Sox's season record fell to 18-26, continuing struggles with scoring.

title: Report: Reunion between Patriots, Stefon Diggs viewed as unlikely despite mutual interest
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Stefon Diggs is open to returning to the Patriots, but an imminent A.J. Brown trade complicates the possibility.
Diggs had a 1,045-yard receiving season with the Patriots last year but would face a salary cap issue with Brown's trade.
Albert Breer suggests that a Diggs return is unlikely due to potential role limitations and salary cap constraints with Brown.

title: Boston police seek missing boy, 15
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Lafayette LaGrant-Gomes, 15, was last seen Tuesday near Blue Hill Avenue in Dorchester, reported missing on Wednesday.
Boston police describe LaGrant-Gomes as a Black male, 5'8", thin build, brown eyes, black hair; familiar with several Boston areas.
Detectives from Boston's District B-3 are investigating his disappearance; public tips can be submitted via 911 or CrimeStoppers.

title: Police: Teen allegedly shot girlfriend’s mother in the head on Mother’s Day
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Tyler Broomes, 18, allegedly shot his girlfriend's mother and another man on May 10 in Dorchester.
The victims, both 42, suffered a headshot and non-life-threatening injuries during the shooting at 69 Kingsdale St.
Broomes faces charges including assault with intent to murder; he is held without bail pending a May 21 hearing.

title: How would a team made up entirely of former Red Sox look in 2026?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Former Red Sox pitchers Chris Sale, Eduardo Rodriguez, and Nathan Eovaldi have a combined 2.74 ERA in 2026.
Mookie Betts and Xander Bogaerts, ex-Red Sox players, continue to perform strongly for their current teams.
Dominic Smith, a former Red Sox player, has emerged as a breakout star, batting over .350 in 2026.

title: Red Sox’ Roman Anthony no longer wearing wrist brace, played catch Friday
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox's Roman Anthony, recovering from a wrist sprain, has removed his brace and played catch on Friday.
Anthony remains on the injured list since May 7 and hasn't resumed swinging a bat but is making progress.
Interim manager Chad Tracy hopes Anthony can start swinging soon during their time in Atlanta.

title: Jeep drives into Norwood day care
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A Jeep accidentally drove into BrightPath Norwood Child Care Center; no injuries reported among children, staff, or the driver.
The incident occurred at 1210 Providence Highway, Norwood; the daycare is temporarily closed for structural damage assessment.
Driver mistakenly hit the accelerator; the crash sent the vehicle into the building's glass front entryway.

title: Here’s what experts are predicting the Patriots’ 2026 record will be
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Experts predict the New England Patriots will finish the 2026 season with a 10-7 record, down from their 14-3 record in 2025.
The 2026 schedule includes challenging opponents like Seattle Seahawks and Kansas City Chiefs, contributing to the predicted lower win total.
Predictions from platforms like The Athletic, ESPN, and FOX Sports reflect a consensus view of the Patriots facing a tougher season.
